Utterance: hello, hi, hey, greetings
Response: Hello! 🌾 I am your ShambaIQ AI Farm Assistant. How can I help you with your crops, livestock, or farm management today?

Utterance: why are my tomato leaves turning yellow, yellow spots on leaves
Response: Yellow leaves can be caused by nitrogen deficiency, overwatering, or early blight fungus. Check your soil moisture and consider applying a balanced fertilizer or fungicide.

Utterance: how to control fall armyworm, worms eating my maize
Response: Handpick caterpillars if infestation is small. For severe attacks, apply recommended systemic insecticides early in the morning or late evening when caterpillars are active.

Utterance: how to do a soil test, how to improve my soil fertility
Response: Collect soil samples from different parts of your farm at a depth of 6-8 inches and take them to a local lab. This helps you know exactly which nutrients are missing.

Utterance: how often should i water my crops, best time to irrigate
Response: Water deeply early in the morning or late in the evening to reduce evaporation loss. The frequency depends on your crop type and soil moisture retention.

Utterance: my potatoes have dark spots, how to treat late blight
Response: Late blight spreads fast in cool, wet weather. Destroy infected plants immediately and apply copper-based fungicides to protect healthy crops.

Utterance: black insects on my beans, how to kill bean aphids
Response: Aphids suck sap and stunt growth. You can spray them with soapy water for a mild infestation or use an organic neem oil spray to control them naturally.

Utterance: how to make organic compost, best manure for farming
Response: Layer dry leaves, green farm waste, and animal manure in a pit. Keep it moist and turn it every 2 weeks. It will decompose into rich organic fertilizer within 3 months.

Utterance: my chickens are coughing and sneezing, poultry disease signs
Response: Respiratory signs can indicate Newcastle disease or Infectious Coryza. Isolate sick birds immediately, keep the coop well-ventilated, and consult a local vet for vaccination options.

Utterance: what is the correct spacing for planting maize
Response: For optimal yields, space your rows 75 cm apart, and leave 25 cm between individual seeds within the row.

Utterance: holes in my cabbage leaves, diamondback moth control
Response: Holes are usually caused by cabbage looper worms or diamondback moth larvae. Spray organic Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) or use neem extracts early in the cycle.

Utterance: how to increase milk production in dairy cows
Response: Ensure cows have access to clean water 24/7, feed them high-quality silage or dairy meal blocks, and maintain a calm, stress-free milking routine.

Utterance: my onions are rotting from the roots, downy mildew onion
Response: This is often white rot or downy mildew caused by poor soil drainage. Avoid planting onions in the same plot next season and ensure rows are well-drained.

Utterance: banana leaves turning yellow and dying, xanthomonas wilt
Response: This looks like Banana Xanthomonas Wilt (BXW) or Panama disease. Sterilize farm tools after working on each plant and cut down infected stems to halt the spread.

Utterance: how to stop soil erosion on a hill, sloping farm tips
Response: Plant deep-rooted cover crops, construct terraces, and plant grass strips like vetiver or Napier grass along the contour lines to hold the soil.

Utterance: best way to kill weeds without chemicals
Response: Practice mulching with straw or plastic sheets to block sunlight, use shallow shallow hand-hoeing, or plant cover crops like sweet potatoes to choke out weeds.

Utterance: my goat has running stomach, treating goat diarrhea
Response: Diarrhea can be caused by internal worms or sudden feed changes. Deworm your goats regularly every 3 months and isolate them to prevent bacterial transmission.

Utterance: tomato plant drying up suddenly, bacterial wilt symptoms
Response: Bacterial wilt causes sudden drying while the leaves are still green. There is no chemical cure. Remove the plant and avoid planting nightshades (potatoes, peppers) there for 3 years.

Utterance: sticky white spots on lemon tree leaves, citrus scale
Response: This is caused by scale insects or mealybugs that excrete honeydew. Spray the tree with a mix of water and horticultural oil or liquid dish soap.

Utterance: why should i mulch my farm beds
Response: Mulching retains soil moisture, suppresses weed growth, regulates soil temperature, and adds organic nutrients back into the dirt as it decomposes.

Utterance: what is the best feed formula for pigs
Response: Pigs need a balance of energy, protein, vitamins, and minerals. Mix maize bran, soybean meal, fish meal, and vitamin premixes. Always provide unlimited clean drinking water.

Utterance: orange spots under coffee leaves, coffee leaf rust
Response: This is coffee leaf rust fungus. Prune branches to improve airflow and sunlight penetration, and spray copper-based fungicides before the rainy season starts.

Utterance: how to start a rabbit farming business, rabbit cage size
Response: Rabbits need clean wire-mesh cages off the ground to prevent disease. Keep breeding does separate, feed them hay and pellets, and ensure cages stay completely dry.

Utterance: avocado tree leaves dropping and dying, root rot disease
Response: Avocado root rot thrives in waterlogged soil. Avoid overwatering, ensure excellent drainage, and use certified disease-free seedlings for new plantings.

Utterance: brown circles on my spinach leaves, cercospora leaf spot
Response: This is Cercospora leaf spot fungus. Avoid overhead watering to keep leaves dry, space your plants widely for airflow, and remove affected leaves.

Utterance: my sheep is limping, how to treat foot rot
Response: Foot rot is a bacterial infection from muddy ground. Trim hoops regularly, keep animal pens dry, and use a copper sulfate foot bath to disinfect their hooves.

Utterance: how to store seeds for next planting season
Response: Dry seeds thoroughly in the sun until they click when bitten. Store them in airtight glass jars or tins in a cool, dark, dry place away from pests.

Utterance: bottom of my bell peppers turning black, blossom end rot
Response: Blossom-end rot is caused by calcium deficiency, often triggered by uneven watering. Maintain consistent watering habits and add agricultural lime to the soil.

Utterance: how to attract bees to a new beehive
Response: Smear melted beeswax or a few drops of lemongrass oil inside the hive entrance. Ensure the hive is hung in a quiet, shaded area close to flowering plants.

Utterance: green flies under my kales, sukuma wiki pest treatment
Response: Those are aphids. You can spray them with an eco-friendly mixture of water, neem oil, and a few drops of mild liquid soap to control them safely.

Utterance: best crops to plant during dry seasons, drought resistant
Response: Plant hardy, drought-tolerant crops like sorghum, millet, cassava, green grams (beans), or sweet potatoes that require very little rainfall to mature.

Utterance: seedlings falling over and dying in nursery, damping off
Response: Damping-off fungus attacks seedlings in cold, overcrowded, overwatered soil. Use clean potting soil, don't overwater, and thin out seedlings to allow airflow.

Utterance: how to get rid of ticks on cows, cattle dip frequency
Response: Ticks spread East Coast Fever. Spray or dip your cattle once a week using recommended acaricides, and keep bushes cleared around pastures.

Utterance: worms inside my mangoes, how to stop fruit flies
Response: Fruit flies lay eggs inside developing fruit. Hang pheromone fruit-fly traps in the trees and collect and bury any dropped, rotting mangoes at least 2 feet deep.

Utterance: what does NPK stand for on fertilizer bags
Response: N is Nitrogen (for leaf growth), P is Phosphorus (for root development), and K is Potassium (for fruit strength and disease resistance).

Utterance: how to plant napier grass for dairy cows
Response: Cut Napier grass stems into pieces with 3 nodes. Plant them at a 45-degree angle in well-manured soil, leaving one node above the ground.

Utterance: can i raise ducks without a swimming pond
Response: Yes, ducks only need enough deep, clean water to submerge their entire heads to clear their eyes and nostrils. A simple plastic basin is perfectly sufficient.

Utterance: why are my watermelons splitting open on the vine
Response: Splitting happens when a dry spell is followed by heavy watering or rain. The fruit absorbs water too fast and bursts. Keep watering schedules steady.

Utterance: how to preserve green maize stalks as silage
Response: Chop green maize into small pieces, pack it tightly into a pit silo to squeeze out all air, cover it with heavy plastic sheeting, and weigh it down with dirt.

Utterance: how to protect delicate vegetables from cold frost
Response: Cover rows with clear plastic crop tunnels or light burlap fabric overnight to trap ground heat, and uncover them during the day so they can absorb sunlight.
